answer: There are 310 open tickets right now: 12 urgent, 47 high, 188 normal, and 63 low. The per-priority breakdown is in the structured payload.

citations.snippet: REST: GET /tickets?status=open&fields=ticket_id,priority (paginated, 310 rows fetched); server grouped by priority and computed count(ticket_id) per group deterministically.

routing_decision.rationale: The question is a record-count rollup of tickets filtered by status and grouped by priority. The support-desk domain owns Ticket via the support-rest backend. REST has no native aggregation, so the server fetched the matching ticket rows (status=open, projecting only ticket_id and priority) and reduced them server-side into a deterministic count per priority. No other domain owns ticket data, so routing stayed single-domain.

alternative_routings.reason: Single-domain routing: support-rest returns the filtered ticket rows and the server computes count(ticket_id) grouped by priority deterministically after fetching.
